name: Loop 13: Col d'Osquich – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 13 : Col d’Osquich — Cols du Pays basque
description:
This 60 kilometer loop crosses emblematic villages of the Basque Country. In between, the Col d'Osquich allows you to take a little height and enjoy pretty views of the region.

It is in the former capital of Navarre, in Saint-Palais, that the route begins. You embark in the direction of Mauléon-Licharre on a road with little traffic. Hilly, it crosses at the foot of Oïhergaïn, Leizeaga Mendi and Agerrea. You discover Mauléon-Licharre by entering its lower town separated from the upper by the Saison.

The section that leads to Musculdy is relatively flat and allows you to regain your strength before tackling the ascent of the Col d'Osquich. Short and without real difficulty, the climb leads to a pretty plateau. The restaurant located there allows you to take a pleasant break.

The descent follows the foothills of several peaks that brighten up this portion. You then find a wide road that slips to Uhart-Mixe. After the village, a last small bump separates you from the finish in Saint-Palais.
region_intro: Hiking around Saint-Palais offers diverse landscapes characterized by rolling hills, lush forests, and tranquil river valleys. The region is situated at the crossroads of three Basque provinces, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Notable features include the Pyrenean foothills and significant historical pilgrimage routes, such as sections of the Camino de Santiago. These paths lead through a mix of natural settings and cultural landmarks. Best hiking trails around Saint-Palais The most popular hiking route is Chapelle de Soyarza – Gibraltar Stele loop from Manano, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views from Chapelle de Soyarza. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gibraltar Stele – Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Saint-Palais, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This trail connects two significant landmarks, providing varied terrain and historical context. Local hikers also love the Chapelle de Soyarza – Gibraltar Stele loop from Amendeuix-Oneix, a 10.3 miles (16.6 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 51 minutes. Hiking around Saint-Palais is defined by rolling hills, lush forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks in the Pyrenean foothills. The routes in Saint-Palais are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Saint-Palais's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Saint-Palais?
A: There are 17 hiking trails around Saint-Palais. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from easy strolls through river valleys to moderate treks in the rolling hills and Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Saint-Palais?
A: Yes, Saint-Palais offers 5 easy hiking trails, perfect for families or beginners. These routes typically wind through lush forests and tranquil river valleys, providing a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24645036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele – Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Tambourindéguia&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 4.3 km path.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Saint-Palais?
A: Hiking in Saint-Palais offers diverse landscapes, including lush forests, rolling hills, and tranquil river valleys. You'll also encounter sections of historic pilgrimage routes and views of the Pyrenean foothills, providing a picturesque backdrop for your adventures.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, several hiking trails in Saint-Palais incorporate significant landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/930773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele&lt;/a&gt;. These points are often included in popular loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1609947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza – Gibraltar Stele loop from Manano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Saint-Palais?
A: The trails in Saint-Palais are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the historical significance of routes like the Camino de Santiago, and the rewarding panoramic views from spots like Chapelle de Soyarza.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Saint-Palais?
A: Yes, many of the hiking routes around Saint-Palais are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6979875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele – Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Saint-Palais&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular path that connects two significant landmarks.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Saint-Palais?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, generally, the natural landscapes of Saint-Palais, with its forests and river valleys, are welcoming for dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved, especially near livestock or other hikers.

Q: Is there public transport access to the trailheads in Saint-Palais?
A: Saint-Palais is a regional hub, and while some trailheads might be accessible directly from the town center, public transport options to more remote starting points can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to reach specific trailheads.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Saint-Palais?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Saint-Palais, particularly near the town center or at designated starting points for popular trails. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24657234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Palais loop from Saint-Palais&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking within the town itself.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Saint-Palais?
A: The region around Saint-Palais is beautiful for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring is particularly lovely when wildflowers are in bloom, and the weather is mild. Autumn also offers pleasant conditions with colorful foliage. Summers can be warm, while winter hiking is possible, though some paths might be muddy or require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in the Saint-Palais area?
A: While Saint-Palais primarily features easy to moderate trails, some routes offer more elevation gain as they venture into the Pyrenean foothills. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza – Gibraltar Stele loop from Amendeuix-Oneix&lt;/a&gt; is a longer moderate trail that covers 16.6 km and includes significant ascents, providing a more demanding experience.

Q: Are there any historical routes or pilgrimage paths I can hike in Saint-Palais?
A: Yes, Saint-Palais is an important stop on historic pilgrimage routes, including the Via Lemovicensis and the Via Podiensis, both integral parts of the Camino de Santiago. Hiking these paths offers a rich historical and cultural journey through the French countryside, blending natural beauty with heritage.
tips:
The small village of Saint-Palais is located at the crossroads of the three Basque provinces of Labourd, Basse Navarre and Soule.
Former capital of Navarre, the village has managed to preserve its traditions and its authentic culture.

Authentic Basque village, Mauléon-Licharre is considered the capital of the espadrille. It is also that of the small province of the Basque Country, Soule. The heights of its old town are separated from the lower town by the course of the Season. We find in this part the castle of Andurain de Maytie.
A small pass to climb on a fine day, a restaurant on the way up, with a magnificent view of the Soule below.
